title: trifolium sylvaticum
intro: Trifolium sylvaticum, also known as woodland clover, is a perennial legume native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its trifoliate leaves and pinkish-white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Trifolium sylvaticum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Trifolium sylvaticum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs in traditional folk medicine.
